proven.claim: Claim: projectRoot is always a non-empty string at line 55.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; Claim: projectRoot is always a non-empty string at line 55.
; Branch A: path.resolve(explicit) returns an absolute path >= 1 char for any input.
; Branch B: findProjectRoot(cwd) returns cwd or an ancestor — both non-empty absolute paths.
(declare-const has_explicit_arg Int)    ; 1 = non-flag arg found in args, 0 = not
(declare-const projectRoot_length Int)  ; string length of projectRoot

; Transition (Branch A): explicit arg present → path.resolve(explicit) is called.
; path.resolve() normalises to absolute; even resolve("") = process.cwd() which is >= 1 char.
(assert (=> (= has_explicit_arg 1) (>= projectRoot_length 1)))

; Transition (Branch B): no explicit arg → findProjectRoot(process.cwd()) is called.
; process.cwd() is an OS-guaranteed non-empty absolute path.
; findProjectRoot returns cwd or a found ancestor — both satisfy length >= 1.
(assert (=> (= has_explicit_arg 0) (>= projectRoot_length 1)))

; Exhaustive case split over the two branches
(assert (or (= has_explicit_arg 0) (= has_explicit_arg 1)))

; Negate the property: can projectRoot_length be 0?
(assert (= projectRoot_length 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — projectRoot is always non-empty at line 55

violations.claim: findProjectRoot fallback: returns startDir when no marker found in any ancestor.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; findProjectRoot fallback: returns startDir when no marker found in any ancestor.
; projectRoot at line 55 can be a directory with no .git, .neurallog, or package.json.
(declare-const explicit_provided Int)       ; 0 = no non-flag arg; Branch B taken
(declare-const traversal_depth Int)         ; number of dirname() steps executed
(declare-const marker_found Int)            ; 1 = .git/.neurallog/package.json found, 0 = not
(declare-const projectRoot_has_structure Int) ; 1 = valid project root, 0 = bare directory

; Transition: no explicit arg → resolveProjectRoot calls findProjectRoot(process.cwd())
(assert (= explicit_provided 0))

; Transition: loop ran at least one iteration (startDir was not filesystem root)
(assert (> traversal_depth 0))

; Transition: statSync threw ENOENT for all three candidate names at every level.
; The catch block runs continue; no return statement was hit.
(assert (= marker_found 0))

; Transition: when marker_found = 0, findProjectRoot exits the while loop and executes
;   return startDir;
; startDir was already checked (first iteration) and had no markers → no project structure.
(assert (=> (= marker_found 0) (= projectRoot_has_structure 0)))

; Violation: line 55 logs startDir as "projectRoot" but it contains no project structure.
(assert (= projectRoot_has_structure 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — projectRoot can be a bare directory at line 55

violations.claim: Semantic correctness violation: "Initializing in X" is logged before X is validated.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Semantic correctness violation: "Initializing in X" is logged before X is validated.
; Transition: lines 52-54 execute before line 55 (resolveProjectRoot, createDefault, first log).
; Transition: new DiffAnalyzer(projectRoot) is constructed at line 58, after line 55.
; Transition: diff.isGitRepo() is called at line 59, after line 55.
(declare-const log_executes_at_line Int)     ; = 55
(declare-const git_check_executes_at_line Int) ; = 59
(declare-const is_git_repo Int)              ; 1 = valid git repo, 0 = not

; Transition: code ordering — log fires before the git guard
(assert (= log_executes_at_line 55))
(assert (= git_check_executes_at_line 59))
(assert (< log_executes_at_line git_check_executes_at_line))

; Consequence of ordering: at line 55, no code has yet evaluated is_git_repo.
; is_git_repo is unconstrained by any executed transition.

; Violation: line 55 executes even when projectRoot is not a git repository.
(assert (= is_git_repo 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — "Initializing in X" prints unconditionally, even for non-git directories

violations.claim: Reachable: multiple non-flag arguments are supplied; only the first is used.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; Reachable: multiple non-flag arguments are supplied; only the first is used.
; Transition: args.find(a => !a.startsWith("-")) returns the FIRST matching element.
; Transition: no validation of args.length or count of non-flag args occurs before line 55.
(declare-const num_non_flag_args Int)  ; count of args without "-" prefix
(declare-const args_used Int)          ; count of non-flag args actually consumed
(declare-const args_silently_dropped Int) ; num_non_flag_args - args_used

; Transition: user passes two non-flag args: neurallog init dir1 dir2
(assert (= num_non_flag_args 2))

; Transition: find() returns first match → exactly one non-flag arg is consumed
(assert (= args_used 1))

; Transition: args_silently_dropped = num_non_flag_args - args_used
(assert (= args_silently_dropped (- num_non_flag_args args_used)))

; Violation: at line 55, one or more user-supplied paths were dropped without error or warning.
; The user intended multiple paths; only one was used; no diagnostic is emitted.
(assert (> args_silently_dropped 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — extra non-flag args are silently ignored; line 55 gives no indication
